Document 1: amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act to revise requirements for reinvestigations of disputed information
establishing a new right to appeal, free of charge, completed disputes adverse to the consumer.
Document 2: requires the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau...to accompany it with all studies, data, and other analyses on which it was based.
Document 4: Amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act to permit a consumer reporting agency to furnish a consumer report in connection with any credit or insurance transaction
imposes limits upon increases of interest rates and finance charges on a credit card account before its scheduled renewal date.
Document 5: amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act with respect to an item of adverse information about an active duty military consumer
requires a consumer reporting agency to notify promptly an active duty military consumer whenever it receives an item of adverse information about him or her.

topic.name: National Wilderness Designation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at designating specific lands as wilderness areas within the National Wilderness Preservation System.

Document 1: establishes the Bronzeville-Black Metropolis National Heritage Area
the authority of the Department of the Interior to provide financial assistance
Document 2: designates specified lands in Maine within the Cross Island National Wildlife Refuge
as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System
Document 3: designates certain federal land in the Olympic National Forest as wilderness
as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System
Document 4: amends the Washington State Wilderness Act of 1984
prohibits the Secretary of Agriculture from moving the Lookout
Document 5: designates specified lands in Utah as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System
sets forth administrative provisions regarding the exchange of state-owned lands
Document 6: designates certain land in the Mount Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System
amends the Wild and Scenic Rivers Act
Document 7: designates specified federal lands in the Monongahela National Forest as wilderness
modifies the boundary of the Laurel Fork South Wilderness

topic.name: Mortgage Regulation and Consumer Protection
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures aimed at regulating mortgage lending practices and enhancing consumer protections in the mortgage industry.

Document 1: Requires a mortgage servicer to assign one case manager to each borrower that seeks a mortgage loan modification or an alternative to foreclosure.
Prohibits a servicer from initiating a covered foreclosure action against a borrower unless the servicer has performed specified tasks.
Document 2: Creates a safe harbor from lawsuit for creditors that are depository institutions for any failure to comply with certain requirements with respect to a residential mortgage loan.
A safe harbor from lawsuit is also created for mortgage originators for steering a consumer to a residential mortgage loan if certain conditions are met.
Document 3: Prohibits a creditor or mortgage broker from arranging a consumer credit transaction secured by the principal dwelling of a consumer without first verifying the consumer's reasonable ability to make the scheduled payments.
Prohibits prepayment penalties and limits finance points, charges, and fees.
Document 4: Requires the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to authorize the participation of mortgage brokers and lenders meeting certain criteria as direct endorsement mortgagees.
Document 5: Prohibits collecting a fee from a veteran for a housing loan, unless the loan is used to refinance indebtedness.
Increases the maximum loan guaranty amount of the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation conforming limit.
Document 6: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to requirements for disclosure to a consumer of points and fees information about a consumer credit transaction.
Excludes from the computation of points and fees any escrow for future payment of insurance.
Document 7: Exempts from maintenance of mortgage loan records and disclosure requirements depository institutions that have originated in each of the two preceding calendar years fewer than 100 closed-end mortgage loans.

topic.name: Medicaid and Medicare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative amendments related to Medicaid and Medicare, addressing issues such as provider payments, prescription drug costs, and eligibility requirements.

Document 1: impose a continuous levy on payments to Medicaid providers
delinquent taxes owed by such providers
Document 2: allow qualifying states to use all or any portion of their allotments under SCHIP for certain Medicaid expenditures
Requires qualifying states to implement at least three of the following policies
Document 3: reduce the costs of prescription drugs for enrollees of Medicaid managed care organizations
payment for covered outpatient drugs dispensed to Medicaid-eligible individuals
Document 4: cover comprehensive Alzheimer's disease and related dementia diagnosis and services
Document 5: provide for optional Medicaid coverage of certain prostate cancer patients
Document 6: enhanced penalties for Medicare Advantage-Prescription Drug plans
public notification about plans that habitually receive intermediate sanctions
Document 7: authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services to negotiate with drug manufacturers
prices charged to prescription drug plan sponsors

topic.name: Regulatory Framework for Commerce
topic.description: This cluster addresses various regulatory measures and laws affecting business activities, competition, and consumer protection in commerce.

Document 1: amends the Clayton Act with respect to actions brought by the Attorney General for violations of antitrust laws
prohibition against acquisition by one corporation of the stock of another that may substantially lessen competition
Document 2: expands the prohibition against state taxation of interstate commerce
defines 'physical presence in a state' for taxation purposes
Document 3: makes it unlawful for providers of electronic communication services to engage in unfair methods of competition
authorizes the Federal Communications Commission to define acts constituting unfair competition
Document 4: amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to revise the prohibition on importing convict-made goods
requires the Federal Trade Commission to enforce this prohibition as if it were an unfair or deceptive act
Document 5: prohibits a state from imposing any tax on electronic commerce unless that person has a physical presence in the state
sets forth criteria for determining physical presence
Document 6: requires disclosure of information by prepaid telephone calling service providers
makes it unlawful to provide fewer minutes than promoted
Document 7: extends the ban on state and local taxation of Internet access
grandfather provisions allowing states to enforce laws imposing a tax on internet access prior to October 1, 1998

topic.name: Pension and Retirement Plan Legislation
topic.description: This topic covers various legislative amendments and acts related to pension plans, retirement accounts, and employee benefits, particularly in the context of commercial airlines and small businesses.

Document 1: employees of commercial passenger airlines who receive payments from a bankruptcy case
rollover such payments into an individual retirement account (IRA)
Document 2: Pension Protection Act ERISA Amendments of 2008
modifies requirements with respect to crediting and termination of surcharges
Document 3: penalty-free distributions from an individual retirement account (IRA)
loans to a small business to purchase depreciable property
Document 4: length of service award plans for bona fide volunteers
exempting a length of service award program from treatment as an employee pension benefit plan
Document 5: require commercial passenger airline pension plan sponsors
use an interest rate of 8.25% in determining the funding target
Document 6: Charitable Pension Flexibility Act of 2013
allows the sponsor of a multiple-employer defined benefit pension plan to elect for a plan to cease to be treated as an eligible charity plan
Document 7: prohibits a former Member of Congress or former congressional employee who is a registered lobbyist
from being eligible for any benefits under the Civil Service Retirement System (CSRS)

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing accountability, risk assessment, and certification within the financial sector.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to require the risk-based assessment system to consider risks posed to the Deposit Insurance Fund.
Directs the FDIC to impose a systemic risk assessment on all systemically important depository institutions.
Document 2: Establishes a Federal Board of Certification to certify that the mortgages within a security instrument meet underlying standards.
The purpose of this certification process is to increase the transparency, predictability, and reliability of securitized mortgage products.
Document 3: Authorizes the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to examine the methodologies used by nationally recognized statistical rating organizations.
Defines asset backed security as one that is primarily serviced by the cash flows of a discrete pool of receivables or other financial assets.
Document 4: Repeals the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act, and revives or restores those provisions of law amended by it.
Revives regulations that were in place prior to the enactment of the Dodd-Frank Act.
Document 5: Exempts specified advisers of small business investment companies from certain SEC registration requirements.
Provides the same exemption with respect to any state or local law requiring the registration of investment advisers.
Document 6: Amends the Dodd-Frank Act to revise the prohibition against federal government assistance to bailouts of registered swap dealers.
Extends the meaning of 'covered depository institution' to include an uninsured U.S. branch or agency of a foreign bank.
Document 7: Prohibits a federal banking regulator from terminating deposit insurance for institutions serving marijuana-related businesses.
Grants immunity from federal criminal prosecution to institutions providing financial services to legitimate marijuana businesses.

topic.name: Child Welfare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures aimed at improving child welfare, adoption processes, and the rights of parents and children in the child welfare system.

Document 1: Protecting Adoption and Promoting Responsible Fatherhood Act of 2009
improve the protection of the rights of responsible unwed fathers
Document 2: Strengthening Child Welfare Response to Trafficking Act of 2015
identify and assess reports involving children who are sex trafficking victims
Document 3: Keeping Families Safe Act of 2008
foster care maintenance payments made on behalf of a child
Document 4: Resource Family Recruitment and Retention Act of 2008
ensure that children in foster care placements in public or private agencies receive quality services
Document 5: Child Welfare Provider Inclusion Act of 2015
prohibits the federal government from discriminating against child welfare service providers
Document 6: mandatory revocation of passports of individuals who are more than $2,500 in arrears in child support payments
Document 7: Speak Out to Stop Child Abuse Act
require the state plan under a grant from the Secretary of Health and Human Services for child abuse and neglect prevention

topic.name: Federal Property Management and Utilization
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative measures related to the management, utilization, and protection of federal properties and resources.

Document 1: Codifies a national security exemption from emissions regulations for excess personal property of the Department of Defense (DOD) that is transferred under specified DOD property transfer programs to a firefighting agency
regulations for excess personal property of the Department of Defense (DOD) that is transferred under specified DOD property transfer programs
Document 3: Directs the Administrator of General Services (GSA) to convey to the National Women's History Museum, Inc. (the Museum) the northern part of specified property in the District of Columbia
Grants the Administrator the authority to convey the southern part of such property to the Museum
Document 4: Vests title to real property and equipment acquired using funds provided by a grant under the Alaska Kiln Drying Grant Program
in accordance with the compensation provisions in this Act.
Document 5: Directs the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), in coordination with the General Services Administration (GSA), to submit a report on the DHS headquarters consolidation project
a comprehensive assessment of the real property needed by DHS in the Region to carry out its mission
Document 6: Directs the Administrator of General Services to install a photovoltaic system, as set forth in the Sun Wall Design Project
Authorizes funding from the Federal Buildings Fund.
Document 7: Directs the head of a federal agency constructing a building at an estimated cost exceeding $1 million
to ensure that the building's life-cycle cost is considered with respect to the building's design.

topic.name: Wild Horse Management
topic.description: Legislation and regulations governing the management and protection of wild horses and burros in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Wild Free-Roaming Horses and Burros Act to revise provisions concerning the management of such animals.
Requires the Secretary of the Interior to ensure that acreage available for wild and free-roaming horses and burros is at least equal to the acreage where they were found in 1971.
Document 2: Amends the Wild Free-Roaming Horses and Burros Act to direct the Department of the Interior and the Department of Agriculture to allow a state or an Indian tribe to assume all management and protection functions.
Document 3: Amends the Wild Free-Roaming Horses and Burros Act to direct the Department of the Interior and the Department of Agriculture to allow a state or an Indian tribe to assume all management and protection functions.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to enter into an agreement with the Corolla Wild Horse Fund to provide for the management of free-roaming wild horses.
Requires that such agreement allow a herd of no more than 130 free-roaming wild horses in and around the Refuge.

topic.name: U.S. Trade Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various U.S. trade laws and amendments aimed at regulating imports, providing duty-free treatment, and addressing trade-related issues with foreign countries.

Document 1: Amends the Trade Act of 1974 to extend duty-free treatment under the Generalized System of Preferences (GSP)
Extends through FY2012 preferential treatment for apparel articles assembled in one or more beneficiary countries
Document 2: Amends the Andean Trade Preference Act to include Paraguay on the list of countries eligible for duty-free treatment
Terminates the extension of such treatment to Paraguayan products after December 31, 2009
Document 3: Directs the U.S. Trade Representative (USTR) to report to Congress annually on the estimated annual loss of revenue to holders of U.S. intellectual property rights
Requires the USTR to impose duties on merchandise from China
Document 4: Directs the FDA, CPSC, EPA, and NHTSA to require foreign manufacturers to register an agent in the United States
Subjects to certain penalties an importer who fails to provide this or files a false declaration
Document 5: Directs the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) to provide for the liquidation or reliquidation (refund of duties) on certain entries of orange juice from Brazil
Prohibits the Secretary of Homeland Security from requiring repayment of duties
Document 6: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to increase the aggregate retail value in the country of shipment of articles that may be imported duty-free
Increases from $200 to $800 for FY2013
Document 7: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to include as a 'countervailable subsidy' requiring action under a countervailing duty or antidumping duty proceeding
Defines 'benefit conferred' in cases where the currency of a foreign country is exchanged for foreign currency.

topic.name: Government Oversight and Policy Coordination
topic.description: This topic encompasses the establishment of commissions and acts aimed at coordinating federal responses and oversight in various sectors, including financial, cybersecurity, and education.

Document 1: Establishes the Financial Oversight Commission to investigate the financial crisis of 2008
Directs the Commission to report to the President and Congress its findings, conclusions, and recommendations.
Document 2: Requires the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP) to establish a committee under the National Science and Technology Council
Directs such committee to coordinate the STEM education activities and programs of federal agencies.
Document 3: Establishes the Commission on Comprehensive Strategies for the Placement of Natural Gas Infrastructure
to conduct a comprehensive review of U.S. natural gas policy to identify problems and make recommendations.
Document 4: Establishes in the Executive Office of the President the National Office for Cyberspace
Requires the Director of the National Office for Cyberspace to oversee information security policies and practices.
Document 5: Establishes the World War I Centennial Commission to plan, develop, and execute programs
serve as a clearinghouse for the collection and dissemination of information about centennial events and plans.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to submit an annual report that summarizes major cyber incidents
Requires similar reports by the DOD Secretary to address incidents against DOD and military departments.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to establish a strategy to prevent the proliferation of weapons of mass destruction
Requires such strategy to include an assessment of gaps in current cooperative nonproliferation efforts.

topic.name: Firearms Legislation and Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures and acts related to the possession, carrying, and regulation of firearms in the United States.

Document 1: Reciprocity for the carrying of certain concealed firearms in different states
Valid state license or permit for carrying a concealed firearm
Document 2: Declare that nothing in it shall authorize or be construed to permit the prohibition of firearm possession
Denies the District any authority to enact laws that discourage private ownership of firearms
Document 4: Require registration of gun show promoters
Submit a report of the transfer to the Attorney General
Document 5: Repeal provisions making it unlawful to possess or discharge a firearm in a school zone
Document 7: Allow licensed firearms dealers to sell or deliver any firearm to a resident of a state other than their own
Eliminate the requirement that a licensee must conduct business at a gun show only in their specified state

topic.name: Federal Recognition and Tribal Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses the processes and implications of federal recognition for Native American tribes, including land trust provisions and eligibility for federal services.

Document 1: Extinguishes all claims and all right, title, and interest in and to specified Indian and non-Indian land
Establishes a tribal trust fund account from which amounts shall be distributed to the Tribes
Document 2: Authorizes the Mescalero Apache Tribe to lease or transfer certain of their water rights
Document 3: Extends federal recognition to the Lumbee Tribe of North Carolina
Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to take land into trust for the Tribe
Document 4: Extends federal recognition to the Duwamish Tribe
Makes the Tribe and its members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government
Document 5: Extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe of Chippewa Indians of Montana
Directs the Secretary to acquire, for the benefit of the Tribe, trust title to 200 acres of land
Document 6: Extends federal recognition to the Duwamish Tribe
Requires the Tribe to conduct an election by secret ballot to adopt a constitution and bylaws for the Tribe
Document 7: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to accept title to any additional acres of real property
The real property taken into trust is not to be eligible, or used, for Class II or III gaming

topic.name: Environmental Regulation and Emission Control
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at regulating air quality and reducing greenhouse gas emissions through various mechanisms such as carbon trading, emission allowances, and the establishment of environmental standards.

Document 1: Establishes the Low Greenhouse Gas Transportation Fund.
Requires states and MPOs to establish goals for reducing greenhouse gas emissions from the transportation sector.
Document 2: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to require the Department of the Treasury to establish a carbon trading program.
This bill establishes a declining cap on the quantity of permits issued to reduce CO2 emissions until 2050.
Document 3: This bill amends the Clean Air Act to require the review of national ambient air quality standards (NAAQS) in eight year intervals.
The EPA may take into account the technological feasibility of attaining NAAQS when revising those NAAQS.
Document 4: Prohibits the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from proposing or finalizing any major rule under the Clean Air Act until after the Administrator completes an economy-wide analysis.
Establishes a process to update such analysis at least semiannually.
Document 5: Requires the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to publish estimates of the total number of preconstruction permits issued by the EPA under the Clean Air Act's New Source Review Program.
The EPA must submit annually a report on actions to expedite the process for review of preconstruction permits.
Document 6: This bill amends subtitle D of the Solid Waste Disposal Act to establish a permit program for coal combustion residuals (coal ash).
The program outlines the criteria that a state must use if it chooses to adopt and enforce a permit program regulating the management and disposal of coal ash.
Document 7: Amends the Clean Air Act to provide for a standard of performance for new electric generating facilities with respect to carbon dioxide emissions.
Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish a carbon dioxide allowance trading program.

topic.name: Veterans and Healthcare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to veterans' benefits, healthcare regulations, and controlled substances management.

Document 1: Allows the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to waive the $6 round-trip deductible paid by veterans
to receive travel costs necessitated by required VA medical care
Document 2: Directs the Commissioner of Food and Drugs (FDA) to take such actions as may be necessary to modify the approval of any drug containing controlled-release oxycodone hychloride
to use for the relief of severe-only pain instead of moderate-to-severe pain
Document 3: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to require any proceedings for the issuance, amendment, or repeal of a rule of the Attorney General
to control a drug or other substance not previously scheduled
Document 4: Amends the Trade Adjustment Assistance Extension Act of 2011 to repeal its revisions to requirements for contracts with Medicare Quality Improvement Organizations (QIOs)
specify expenditures or funding for 3-year QIO contracts
Document 7: Amends the Housing and Economic Recovery Act of 2008 to extend through December 31, 2012, a specified protection for servicemembers against mortgage foreclosure
maintaining through such date the stay of proceedings period of 9 months

topic.name: Regulatory Accountability and Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing the accountability and analysis of regulatory actions by federal agencies, focusing on their economic impact and compliance with established standards.

Document 1: Authorizes the President to require an independent regulatory agency to comply with regulatory analysis requirements applicable to other federal agencies
submit to the Administrator for review any proposed or final significant rule.
Document 2: require regulatory impact analyses for rules that do not involve a legislative mandate
require such agencies to identify and consider regulatory alternatives before promulgating any proposed or final rule
Document 3: Prohibits any federal agency from taking any significant regulatory action until the Bureau of Labor Statistics reports a monthly unemployment rate equal to or less than 7.7%
Authorizes the President to waive such prohibition if the President notifies Congress that a waiver is necessary on the basis of national security or a national emergency.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to establish a program to eliminate duplicative environmental reviews and approvals under state and federal law for rail and highway transportation projects.
Authorizes a state to use state environmental review and approval laws and procedures in lieu of federal environmental laws and regulations.
Document 5: Makes final, and not subject to judicial review, federal agency decisions to issue a permit, license, or approval for a highway or public transportation capital project
prepare supplemental environmental impact statements for projects in cases where the Secretary of Transportation considers new information received after the close the comment period.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2016 the tax credit for research expenditures

require a one-stop delivery system, in selecting and approving training services or programs of training services, to give priority consideration to state- and local board-approved services and programs that lead to an industry-recognized and nationally portable credential.

topic.name: Student Loan Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative amendments and acts aimed at improving student loan processes, repayment options, and financial assistance for borrowers in higher education.

Document 1: requires institutions of higher education (IHEs) to notify their Pell Grant recipients
requires the exit counseling that IHEs provide to student borrowers to include personalized information
Document 2: directs the Secretary of Education to pay the interest that accrues on unsubsidized Federal Family Education Loans
limits these interest-free deferment periods to those occurring on or after this Act's enactment
Document 3: amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to extend the Pell Grant program
forgives the balance due on DLs and direct consolidation loans by individuals who have been public sector employees
Document 4: directs the Secretary of Education to establish a program to refinance the unpaid principal
sets the interest rate on the refinanced loans at the rate on high-yield 10-year Treasury notes plus 1%
Document 5: allows a borrower of a loan under the William D. Ford Federal Direct Loan program to modify the interest rate
fixes that interest rate for the life of the loan
Document 6: modifies the Federal Pell Grant program to increase the maximum award
direct the Department of Education to refinance outstanding federal student loans
Document 7: providing student loan forgiveness or cancellation under the Federal Family Education Loan or Direct Loan programs
limits such forgiveness or cancellation to no more than $5,000 of the borrower's loan outstanding upon completion of five years of service

topic.name: U.S. Territories Legislation
topic.description: Legislation addressing the rights, recognition, and governance of U.S. territories, particularly focusing on historical injustices and economic management.

Document 1: Recognizes the suffering and the loyalty of the people of Guam during the Japanese occupation of Guam in World War II.
Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to make specified payments to living Guam residents who were raped, injured, interned, or subjected to forced labor.
Document 2: Addresses Puerto Rico's debt by establishing an oversight board, a process for restructuring debt, and expedited procedures for approving critical infrastructure projects.
The territories referred to in this bill include Puerto Rico, Guam, American Samoa, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and the U.S. Virgin Islands.
Document 3: Conveys to the government of the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands submerged lands surrounding such Islands and extending three geographical miles outward from their coastlines.
Document 4: Amends the Revised Organic Act of the Virgin Islands to authorize the legislature of the Virgin Islands to create units of local government in the Virgin Islands.
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of the Navy to permit a third party to establish and maintain a memorial to honor members of the Armed Forces who have served as divers.
Document 6: Authorizes the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to sell for fair market value Pershing Hall, in Paris, France.

topic.name: Prescription Drug Policy and Safety
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving prescription drug practices, safety, and access within healthcare systems.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act with respect to prescription drug plans
Grants the Secretary authority similar to that of other federal entities that purchase prescription drugs in bulk to negotiate contracts with manufacturers.
Document 2: Requires the Department of Health and Human Services to provide information to prescribers on best practices for prescribing naloxone
Naloxone is a prescription drug used to rapidly reverse an opioid overdose.
Document 3: Directs the Department of Veterans Affairs to require a VA employee authorized to prescribe controlled substances to complete continuing education on pain management
Courses shall include safe prescribing practices and identification of potential substance use disorders.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the label of a drug containing an opioid to prominently state that addiction is possible
Opioids are drugs with effects similar to opium.
Document 5: Amends the Office of National Drug Control Policy Reauthorization Act to repeal a provision requiring opposition to the legalization of substances in schedule I
Includes marijuana in the context of medical use.
Document 6: Requires a prescription drug plan sponsor to have a payment structure that encourages efficient dispensing
Minimizes the dispensing of unused drugs in long-term care facilities.
Document 7: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to allow a pharmacist to partially fill a prescription for a schedule II controlled substance
The total quantity dispensed in partial fillings must not exceed the total quantity prescribed.

topic.name: Veteran Benefits Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at providing financial relief and support for veterans and their families.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of Veterans Affairs from collecting debts owed to the United States by certain veterans who die as a result of a service-connected disability.
Termination of collection is in the best interest of the United States.
Document 2: Prohibits the Secretary of Veterans Affairs from collecting debts owed to the United States by certain veterans who die as a result of a service-connected disability.
Termination of collection is in the best interest of the United States.
Document 3: Amends the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2008 to back-date to September 11, 2001, the period of active duty.
Members of Ready Reserve components shall receive credit in determining eligibility for early receipt of non-regular service retired pay.
Document 4: Repeals certain provisions which require the offset of amounts paid in dependency and indemnity compensation from Survivor Benefit Plan annuities.
Directs the Secretary concerned to restore annuity eligibility to a surviving spouse.
Document 5: Requires a creditor to provide to a member of the Armed Forces on active regular, reserve, or National Guard duty with respect to any extension of consumer credit.
Directs the Secretary of Defense to prepare a list of DOD financial counseling offices.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Defense to provide transportation on scheduled and unscheduled military flights for veterans with a service-connected disability rated as total.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to assign a 30% temporary disability rating upon separation from the Armed Forces.
States disability compensation payments received are not subject to offset.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative measures and acts related to immigration policies, status adjustments, and penalties for unlawful presence in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits the implementation of any program that grants temporary legal status to individuals unlawfully present in the United States
until the Secretary of Homeland Security certifies that the integrated entry and exit data system has been fully implemented.
Document 2: Establishing Mandatory Minimums for Illegal Reentry Act of 2015 or Kate's Law
increases penalties applicable to aliens who unlawfully reenter the United States after being removed.
Document 3: Indonesian Family Refugee Protection Act
authorizes a qualifying Indonesian citizen whose asylum claim was denied to file a motion to reopen such claim.
Document 4: Liberian Refugee Immigration Fairness Act of 2015
directs the Department of Homeland Security to adjust to permanent resident status a qualifying Liberian national.
Document 5: Accountability in Immigrant Repatriation Act of 2011
amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to direct the Secretary of State to act upon foreign government delays in accepting returning aliens.
Document 6: Grant Residency for Additional Doctors Act of 2014
facilitates expedited review of J-visa applications for aliens seeking graduate medical education.
Document 7: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to eliminate specified immigrant and labor certification petition filing deadlines
for certain aliens physically present in the United States.

topic.name: Housing Assistance Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving housing assistance programs and services for low-income individuals and families in the United States.

Document 1: amends the United States Housing Act of 1937
require local governments or other HUD-approved entities to make inspections and rent determinations
Document 2: amends the United States Housing Act of 1937 to require the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to establish a pilot program
promote the training and employment of public housing residents as home health aides
Document 3: amends the United States Housing Act of 1937 to revise and eliminate specified requirements with respect to public housing agencies
authorize a PHA to request the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to establish a flat rent
Document 4: authorize states and localities receiving emergency assistance for the redevelopment of abandoned and foreclosed homes
renovate single-family housing that is owned and occupied as a principal residence by a family whose income does not exceed 80% of the median income
Document 5: amends the United States Housing Act of 1937 to repeal tenant-based contract renewals
authorize FY2008-FY2012 appropriations for tenant-based rental assistance
Document 6: amends the National Housing Act to increase high-cost area and project-based additional mortgage loan limits
FHA-insured mortgages for rental housing
Document 7: amends the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act to redefine 'homeless'
include a child or youth who has been verified as homeless by a local educational agency

topic.name: Veterans Benefits Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving benefits and support for veterans, including travel allowances, burial benefits, and mental health support.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of Veterans Affairs from imposing any income or pension eligibility requirement for veteran travel reimbursement.
Document 2: Entitles veterans to vocational rehabilitation benefits if hospitalized or receiving outpatient care for a permanent total disability.
Document 3: Includes territorial seas of Vietnam for service connection presumption for veterans exposed to herbicides.
Document 4: Increases burial and funeral expense allowances for deceased veterans.
Document 5: Requires education programs for family members and caregivers of veterans regarding mental health disorders.
Document 6: Requires the Department of Veterans Affairs to pay burial costs for veterans who die in care.
Document 7: Makes veterans eligible for a plot allowance for burial in non-national cemeteries.

topic.name: Federal Grant Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various federal acts that authorize grants for specific community and conservation programs.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to provide a grant to the Denver Museum of Nature & Science
Requires the Museum to match any amounts provided
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants to states
Prescribes program implementation guidelines, including application requirements
Document 3: Amends title IV part B of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants
Sets forth a maximum grant amount to be awarded to a state or political subdivision
Document 4: Expands marine turtle conservation assistance under the Marine Turtle Conservation Act
Authorizes appropriations to the Marine Turtle Conservation Fund
Document 5: Removes the authority of the U.S. Postal Service to issue semipostal stamps
Congress must determine the appropriate agencies or entities that receive such amounts
Document 6: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make grants to public or nonprofit private entities
Directs the Secretary to make an award of a grant or contract for the establishment and operation of a clearinghouse
Document 7: Declares that foreign nongovernmental organizations shall not be ineligible for assistance under the Foreign Assistance Act
Shall not be subject to requirements relating to the use of non-U.S. government funds

topic.name: Nuclear Energy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts aimed at advancing nuclear energy technology, research, and regulatory frameworks in the United States.

Document 1: Nuclear Energy Innovation and Modernization Act
a program to develop the expertise and regulatory processes necessary to allow innovation and the commercialization of advanced nuclear reactors.
Document 2: Nuclear Energy Research and Development Act of 2010
reducing the costs of nuclear reactor systems; reducing used nuclear fuel and nuclear waste products generated by civilian nuclear energy.
Document 3: United States-India Energy Security Cooperation Act of 2007
cooperate with India to address common energy challenges, to ensure future global energy security.
Document 4: American centrifuge project research, development, and demonstration program
